label: Fresh Mint
description: Light Next.js starter with a fresh teal accent, crisp surfaces, and a slightly more product-confident posture than starter-neutral. Best when the prompt sounds like a clean modern Next.js base that wants a hint of brand without committing to a real product yet.
signatureMotif: crisp light surfaces, a fresh teal accent, and product-leaning starter rhythm
promptHints: Treat the page as a clean modern Next.js base with one fresh teal accent — confident but not yet branded.
Stay extension-friendly: prefer flexible neutral surfaces and let the accent carry the freshness instead of a full color story.
Use crisp 1px borders, medium radii, and a calm vertical rhythm; avoid playful textures or oversized marketing chrome.

layouts: Use a centered single-column shell with a slim top bar holding a small wordmark, three nav links, and one outlined primary action on the right.
Build the hero as a left-aligned headline stack with one teal primary action, one ghost link, and an optional small product preview card beside it.
Compose feature areas as a three-card row with equal heights, white surfaces on a tinted page, small icon badges, and short single-paragraph copy.
Lay out docs or settings pages with a narrow sidebar, sticky section anchors, and a calm reading column anchored by consistent row spacing.
Use forms and onboarding screens as a centered white card with a soft teal-tinted halo, single-column fields, and a restrained footer of secondary links.
motifs: Pair near-white cool backgrounds with bright white cards, 1px hairline borders, and a fresh teal primary that reads as a calm brand cue rather than a campaign color.
Keep typography crisp with Geist or Manrope headings, modest size jumps, and clean medium weights so headings feel product-ready without dominating.
Use sparse interface chrome: small rounded chips for status, faint section dividers, and gentle teal focus rings on inputs and primary actions.
Let spacing carry the design — predictable gutters, even card heights, and a soft top-of-page wash that keeps the surface from feeling dead-flat.
antiPatterns: Avoid full-bleed marketing heroes, bold gradient skies, or layered hero illustrations that imply a finished campaign or product launch.
Never lean on dark surfaces, neon accents, or terminal chrome; the freshness comes from light surfaces and a single calm teal.
Avoid stacking multiple accent colors, rainbow chips, or pastel collages that would cost the variant its quiet product-leaning posture.
Do not overload starter pages with dense dashboard widgets, complex data tables, or multi-column app-shell layouts before the prompt clearly asks for them.

themeTokens.bodyBackgroundImage: radial-gradient(circle at top right, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 7%, transparent) 0%, transparent 38%), radial-gradient(circle at bottom left, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-accent) 6%, transparent) 0%, transparent 40%)
